informal: Show that there is no holomorphic function $f$ in the unit disc $D$ that extends continuously to $\partial D$ such that $f(z) = 1/z$ for $z \in \partial D$.
lean:
theorem Shakarchi_exercise_3_22 (D : Set ℂ) (hD : D = ball 0 1) (f : ℂ → ℂ)
    (hf : DifferentiableOn ℂ f D) (hfc : ContinuousOn f (closure D)) :
    ¬ ∀ z ∈ (sphere (0 : ℂ) 1), f z = 1 / z
reasoning: The informal statement says no holomorphic function on the open unit disc, continuous on the closed disc, satisfies f(z)=1/z on the boundary. The Lean signature takes an arbitrary f that is differentiable on D=ball 0 1 and continuous on closure D, and concludes ¬ ∀ z ∈ sphere 0 1, f z = 1/z, i.e., f cannot equal 1/z on the entire unit circle. This is logically equivalent to the non-existence claim: for all such f, the boundary identity fails. The hypotheses (DifferentiableOn = holomorphic on the open disc, ContinuousOn on closure = continuous extension to ∂D) and conclusion (negation of the boundary equation on the unit circle) match the informal statement exactly.
